name: MoneyGram
description_short: MoneyGram Long Beach, California — MoneyGram is a money transfer service with 20+ Long Beach locations offering domestic and international money sending...
description_long:
MoneyGram is an established money transfer company that operates through a network of retail partner locations rather than standalone branches. The company focuses on enabling customers to send and receive money domestically and internationally by leveraging existing retail infrastructure across multiple store types. MoneyGram's service model relies on partnerships with diverse retailers including grocery stores (La Bodega Fresh Market, Big Saver Foods), pharmacies (CVS), general retailers (Walmart), specialty check-cashing services (Ace Cash Express, The Check Cashers), and independent multi-service shops. This distributed network approach allows MoneyGram to maintain a wide geographic footprint without operating dedicated locations.

The platform offers money transfer capabilities both online and offline, with customers able to initiate transfers through MoneyGram's website or by visiting any of their partner locations. The Long Beach market alone shows 20 available service points, demonstrating significant local accessibility. Operating hours vary by location partner, ranging from 3:00 PM to 10:00 PM closings, providing flexibility for different customer schedules. Customers can search for nearby locations by address, city, state, or ZIP code through the company's location finder tool.

MoneyGram's primary distinction is its hybrid online-offline approach combined with its reliance on established retail partners rather than proprietary branches. This model allows them to offer extensive geographic coverage at minimal capital expense while piggybacking on retailers' existing foot traffic and operating infrastructure. The partnership model also enables MoneyGram to serve customers who prefer in-person transaction handling alongside those comfortable with digital transfers.

A key limitation is that MoneyGram functions as a money transfer network rather than a full-service financial institution. The website content provided does not detail fee structures, transfer limits, delivery times, exchange rates for international transfers, or specific regulatory disclosures. Users must visit individual partner locations or the main website to understand pricing and complete service terms. The reliance on third-party retailers means service quality and customer experience may vary significantly by location partner.
address: 1310 Pine Ave Ste A, Long Beach, CA 90813

company_info.headquarters: 1310 Pine Ave Ste A, Long Beach, CA 90813

pros: 20+ locations in Long Beach alone providing extensive local accessibility
Hybrid online and offline transfer options for flexible customer preferences
Partnerships with major retailers (Walmart, CVS) alongside specialized check-cashing services
Extended operating hours through partner network (some locations open until 10:00 PM)
Simple location search by address, city, state, or ZIP code
Both domestic and international money sending and receiving capabilities
No need for customers to maintain separate accounts or relationships with MoneyGram directly
cons: Service quality and wait times dependent on individual retail partner locations
Website provides no fee, exchange rate, or transfer speed information for cost comparison
Reliance on third-party retailers means limited direct customer service control
No information on transfer limits, minimum/maximum amounts, or delivery timeframes
Partner location closures or service interruptions outside MoneyGram's direct control
best_for: Customers seeking in-person money transfer services without opening a dedicated account
International remittance senders who prefer handling cash at retail locations
Individuals in Long Beach needing convenient money transfer access near their home or work
diagnosis: MoneyGram is best for customers who need convenient, in-person money transfer capabilities and prefer the accessibility of retail partner networks over dedicated financial institutions. The primary caveat is that the website lacks critical consumer information—no fee schedules, transfer limits, delivery times, or regulatory disclosures are visible, requiring customers to contact specific locations or visit the main domain for complete service details.
services: In-person money transfer at retail partners
Partnerships with grocery stores for transfer access
Partnerships with pharmacies for transfer access
Partnerships with general retailers for transfer access
Partnerships with check-cashing services for transfer access
